title: Tell Qadesh Dig: Crating the Catalogued Finds
strategies: catalogue-order first-fit: seal each artifact into the lowest-index crate with room in BOTH mass and slots, opening a new crate only when none fits (reproduces the evaluator's weak first-fit reference)
first-fit-decreasing: sort artifacts heaviest-first, then first-fit, so big finds are placed early and light sherds top off partly-filled crates

best-fit-decreasing with local search: place heaviest-first into the tightest-fitting crate, then repeatedly dissolve the weakest (fewest-item) crate by re-homing its contents to cut the crate count further

constraint-aware / metaheuristic packing: balance the mass and slot limits per instance (mass binds for heavy 'half' instances, slots bind for light 'small' instances) and run seeded relocate/swap or perturb-and-reoptimize restarts
